video_goal: Create a 7-15 second TikTok product short that turns the product brief and available source materials into a clear hook, problem, demo, proof, and CTA flow.

strategy_segments.purpose: Stop the scroll by showing the cost or pain point immediately.
strategy_segments.execution_note: Use the strongest pain-point line from the product brief as opening text. Pair with the clearest product or demo visual available.

strategy_segments.purpose: Make the viewer recognize the grooming pain or cost problem.
strategy_segments.caption_direction: Pet nail trims are stressful and expensive.
strategy_segments.execution_note: Use source video moments that show pet handling, grooming setup, or the need for safer trimming.

strategy_segments.purpose: Show the product mechanism or use case clearly.
strategy_segments.caption_direction: LED light helps you see the quick before cutting.
strategy_segments.execution_note: Prioritize close-up product operation. If using the 720x960 source clip, crop or rebuild to 9:16 before final timeline work.

strategy_segments.purpose: Give the viewer confidence that the product is safer, quieter, or easier.
strategy_segments.execution_note: Use product image as a stable proof visual if no dedicated proof clip exists. Keep text short and benefit-led.

strategy_segments.purpose: Tell the viewer what to do next.
strategy_segments.execution_note: End with TikTok Shop-oriented CTA text. Do not publish automatically; this is only strategy output.

material_usage.recommended_use: Use as product clarity or proof visual when video detail is insufficient.
Use for problem, demo, and motion context. Crop or rebuild to vertical if aspect ratio risk exists.
Use for problem, demo, and motion context. Crop or rebuild to vertical if aspect ratio risk exists.
Use only as style or pacing reference, not as final owned footage.
Use for hook wording, captions, CTA, and message constraints.

next_step: Use this strategy to plan a structured timeline. Do not render or edit video in P0_003.
